mcp-servers.purpose: Claude Code MCP servers spawned from .mcp.json (memory, sequential-thinking, filesystem, sqlite, mcos-memory, mcos-contracts, mcos-watcher, mcos-bridge).

sub-agents.purpose: Claude Code sub-agent processes spawned via the Agent tool (architect, sentinel, planner, reviewer, debugger, test-writer, refactorer, documenter, performance-engineer, security-auditor, git-manager, build-resolver, coordinator, multi-file-specialist, plus MCOS-specific agents).

notes: Caps are independent per-pool, not summed. If both pools saturate to 90% the Windows scheduler arbitrates contention; this matches the operator-authorized 'up to 90%' wording.
CPU rate uses JOBOBJECT_CPU_RATE_CONTROL_HARD_CAP so 90% is enforced, not advisory.
Memory cap uses JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_JOB_MEMORY across all processes in the job (combined commit charge).
breakawayOk=false means child processes inherit job membership, so an MCP server that spawns helpers stays inside the cap.
killOnJobClose=false means closing handles does not terminate the workers. Pools persist for the session via the anchor handle held by pool-init.ps1.
If a pool cannot be created or assigned, pool-spawn.ps1 logs WARNING to stderr and continues unenforced. It does NOT silently pretend success (per MCOS rule against fake telemetry).
